title: Read the manifest
prompt: manifest.txt is 41 lines long, and only its very LAST line matters: it names the colored door to enter. Read the end of the file (tail shows the last 10 lines), then look inside that door's folder for the key — it holds the flag. cat, head, and tail read files without opening an editor.

details.restate: Find out which door the last line of manifest.txt names, then read the key file inside that door's folder. The key holds the flag you submit.

io.returns: (the last 10 lines — the final one names the door)
io.because: tail reads the end of a file, which is where this answer lives

terms.meaning: print a whole file / its first 10 lines / its last 10 lines

help.plain: The last line of manifest.txt names a folder. The flag is in that folder's key.txt.
help.analogy: A 41-page briefing where only the last sentence matters — flip to the back, don't read cover to cover.
help.mnemonic: head reads the start, tail reads the end.
help.nudge: Which command shows just the END of a long file?
